method_atom: a conservative discontinuous-Galerkin finite-element scheme for numerically integrating a nonlinear transport (advection) equation on a phase-space mesh coupled self-consistently to a mean field sourced by the transported density; with piecewise-polynomial (including piecewise-constant) basis functions it conserves the total mass, preserves positivity of the transported density, and weakly enforces continuity of the field across element interfaces and boundaries, with a-priori error estimates and verification against known linear damping and nonlinear instability behaviors.
object_atom: the Vlasov-Poisson system: the time-evolving phase-space distribution function of electrons in a collisionless plasma together with its self-consistent electrostatic field, exhibiting linear and nonlinear Landau damping, the two-stream instability, and long-time BGK-like states.
brief_reason: The transferable machinery is the mass/positivity-preserving discontinuous-Galerkin discretization of a transport equation coupled to a self-consistent mean field, while the collisionless-plasma distribution function and its electrostatic field (Landau damping, two-stream instability) are the plasma-physics-specific object.
